Apathy: Why struggle when nothing will ever truly change?
Let your zeal grow cold and quiet.
Nothing matters enough for this weary fight.
Bitterness: Remember every wrong and let it harden you.
Let resentment strengthen what mercy would weaken.
Forgiveness is for fools who enjoy being wounded.
Deceit: A little lie will serve you well today.
Truth is such a narrow, tiresome path.
You only know what I allow you to see.
Despair: There is no hope waiting for you.
Your strength is spent; lie down at last.
Why rise again when failure is certain?
Doubt: Are you sure the promise still holds?
What if the promise fails you now?
Faith is such a fragile little thing.
Envy: Look what others have and what you lack.
Why should they be blessed above you?
Your portion is small, and never enough.
Fear: You know exactly what could happen next.
Run while you still have breath remaining.
Courage will only carry you into pain.
Gluttony: Feed the flesh and call it comfort.
Take more than you need, then more.
Comfort is worth any cost to conscience.
Enmity: Make them your enemy before they strike.
Peace is weakness dressed in holy words.
Let hatred answer where mercy would speak.
Hypocrisy: Say one thing, and live another way.
A holy mask suits your face well.
Appear righteous; that is more than enough.
Idolatry: Bow to what your eyes can see.
This world has better gods to offer.
Give your heart to lesser things gladly.
Legalism: Earn what grace would freely give you.
Measure yourself by rules you cannot keep.
Try harder, Adam; still harder than before.
Lust: Desire deserves a throne in your heart.
Take what your hungry eyes already crave.
Holiness cannot satisfy flesh like this desire.
Malice: Let them suffer as they surely deserve.
Strike with a willing and sharpened heart.
Mercy is wasted on those who hurt you.
Pride: You need no help from anyone above.
Stand tall upon your own greatness now.
Humility is beneath a man like you.
Rebellion: No one has the right to rule you.
Break the yoke and call it freedom.
Commandment is captivity dressed as false holiness.
Sloth: Rest a little longer; duty can wait.
The work can wait until tomorrow morning.
Diligence is exhausting, and ease is sweet.
Vanity: Let them admire what you have become.
Your image is everything worth guarding closely.
Glory in yourself while others look on.
Greed: Keep what you can take for yourself.
More will finally satisfy your hungry heart.
Your neighbor's need is not your burden.
Wrath: Let anger rule before mercy can speak.
Strike first, before pity weakens your hand.
Burn hot and answer harshly today, Adam.
Mammon: Your treasure will not profit you now.
Let me weigh your faith against gold.
Everything has a price, even you, Adam.
Beelzebub: Swat at corruption all you like, little man.
You are surrounded by rot and ruin.
I will make your strength decay within you.
Belial: No master, no law, no judgment for you.
Cast off restraint and be free, Adam.
You fight as though righteousness can save you.
Legion: We are many, and you stand alone.
How will you stand against us all?
A thousand voices rise to accuse you.
Apollyon: Destruction comes for you with open jaws.
I will break what remains of courage.
Your path ends in ruin and silence.
Lucifer: Still pretending your borrowed light is yours?
Your light is borrowed, and fading fast.
I know the weakness beneath your armor.
Dragon: I will sweep you away like chaff.
Your little sword cannot pierce my wrath.
Stand if you must; be consumed utterly.
Fiery Dart: A burning accusation for your guarded heart.
Let this thought pierce you deeply now.
Doubt burns hotter than any earthly flame.
Poison: A little venom will weaken your resolve.
Drink deeply of corruption and hidden bitterness.
Let bitterness do its quiet work within.
Snare: Your feet are caught in my trap.
Struggle all you like; the snare holds.
I have tangled stronger men than you.
Smoke: Let the word grow dim before you.
Can you see clearly through this veil?
A veil for your understanding today, Adam.
Roar: Tremble before the sound of my wrath!
Let fear answer instead of your faith.
Your courage is only noise before me.
Lying Words: Did God really say what you remember?
Truth bends easily in frightened mortal ears.
Let me improve the story for you.
Flame Breath: Burn beneath the heat of my wrath!
The fire is mine to command alone.
Ashes would suit your little faith well.
Flood: The waters rise against your little hope!
Be swallowed by the rising flood now.
I will drown your hope beneath wrath.
Devour: I tire of you, mortal. Feel the power of my wrath!
